blog.own-your-authenticator.the_recent_e: The recent exploitation of the Signaling System No. 7 (SS7) protocol has shifted the security landscape from theoretical risk to industrial emergency. For decades, the SS7 layer—the architectural foundation of global telecommunications—has operated with trust-based protocols that are trivial to intercept via tools like FirstMile. This makes SMS-based 2FA not just obsolete, but a liability for any organization handling sensitive data.
blog.own-your-authenticator.unlike_moder: Unlike modern IP-based protocols, SS7 lacks mutual authentication. An adversary with access to a global roaming exchange can masquerade as a legitimate telecommunications provider, requesting location data or intercepting SMS traffic meant for a specific subscriber. The result is a seamless "SIM Swap" without physical access to the device or cooperation from the carrier.

features.llm-tools.reduce_latency_and_co: Reduce latency and costs by up to 90% with intelligent caching that understands intent.
Reduce latency and costs by up to 90% with intelligent caching that understands intent. Semantically similar queries return cached responses without calling the LLM.
features.llm-tools.route_simple_queries_: Route simple queries to cheaper, faster models and complex reasoning tasks to high-tier models automatically. Optimize cost without sacrificing quality.
features.llm-tools.support_for_openai_an: Support for OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, DeepSeek, Google, and local models. Dynamic routing cuts costs by up to 60%.
features.llm-tools.the_approach_is_calle: The approach is called tool-augmented generation. Instead of asking the LLM to know everything from its training data, you give it tools it can call when needed. Need current stock prices? Call the market data API. Need to check inventory? Query the database. Need to send a confirmation email? Use the mail tool. Each tool is defined in BASIC and registered with the LLM. The model decides when to use each tool based on the user's request. This dramatically reduces hallucinations because the model is pulling real data instead of guessing. It also cuts costs because you can use smaller, cheaper models for most queries and only route complex ones to expensive frontier models.

features.talk-to-data.stop_waiting_for_r: Stop waiting for reports. General Bots enables everyone in your organization to query data using the language they already speak.
features.talk-to-data.the_bot_doesn_t_ju: The bot doesn't just return numbers — it synthesizes trends, highlights anomalies, and provides context for the data. Charts and visualizations generated automatically.
The bot doesn't just return numbers; it synthesizes trends and provides context for the data.
features.talk-to-data.the_system_works_b: The system works by mapping your database schema to business concepts automatically. A table called 'cust_orders' becomes 'customer orders.' A column called 'ord_total' becomes 'order total.' Users don't need to understand foreign keys, join conditions, or aggregate functions. They just ask: 'How many orders did we get last month?' or 'Show me sales by region for Q3.' The AI generates the SQL, executes it against your database, and returns the results as plain language with optional charts. If the result looks wrong, the user can ask follow-up questions to refine. It's like having a data analyst on call 24/7.

features.trainning.a_bot_is_only_as_good: A bot is only as good as its knowledge. If your bot doesn't know your products, policies, and procedures, it will give wrong answers. Training is the process of teaching your bot by feeding it your documents — and General Bots makes this trivially simple.

features.trainning.update_word_or_pdf_do: Update Word or PDF documents and see the bot's knowledge update instantly without manual re-indexing or downtime.
Update Word or PDF documents and see the bot's knowledge update instantly. No manual re-indexing, no downtime, no rebuilds.
features.trainning.you_don_t_need_to_lab: You don't need to label data, create training sets, or fine-tune models. Just drop your documents into a .gbkb folder — PDFs, Word files, Excel spreadsheets, plain text — and the system handles the rest. It automatically chunks the documents into meaningful segments, generates embeddings using your chosen model, and indexes them in Qdrant for fast semantic search. When a user asks a question, the system finds the most relevant chunks and includes them in the prompt to the LLM. If you update a document, the knowledge base updates automatically — no manual re-indexing required. This is true continuous learning: your bot gets smarter as you add more documents.

features.web-automation.think_of_it_as_a: Think of it as a robot that can use a web browser. You define the steps: navigate to this URL, log in with these credentials, click this button, extract that data, fill in this form, submit. The automation runs headlessly (no visible browser window) and can handle complex scenarios like multi-page workflows, file downloads, session management, and CAPTCHA handling. Each automation is defined as a BASIC script, making it easy to write, test, and modify. This is how companies automate supplier onboarding, extract competitor pricing, submit regulatory filings, and sync data between systems that don't talk to each other.

general-bots.llm.one_of_the_strongest_op: One of the strongest open-weight model families available. Qwen 2.5 72B competes with GPT-4 on multiple benchmarks. Available in sizes from 0.5B to 110B. The 32B and 72B variants run efficiently on consumer-grade GPUs. No API costs, no usage limits.

general-bots.llm.the_ai_market_is_domina: The AI market is dominated by a few expensive players — Claude from Anthropic, GPT from OpenAI. They charge premium prices, lock you into their ecosystem, and train on your data. General Bots gives you real alternatives: models that perform just as well for most tasks, at a fraction of the cost. Self-hosted. Private. Open source.
general-bots.llm.the_breakout_model_of_2: The breakout model of 2025. DeepSeek V3 matches GPT-4 on reasoning and coding at roughly 5% of the API cost. DeepSeek R1 introduces chain-of-thought reasoning that rivals o1. Fully open weights — run on your own hardware, no per-token fees, complete data privacy.
general-bots.llm.we_don_t_exclude_the_bi: We don't exclude the big players — we give you the choice to use them when you need them. Run GPT-4 or Claude for complex reasoning, route simple queries to DeepSeek or Qwen. Dynamic model routing lets you mix and match based on cost, latency, and capability requirements. One API, every model, your rules.
general-bots.llm.why_pay_20_user_month_f: Why pay $20/user/month for ChatGPT or $200/user/month for Claude Pro when open-source alternatives deliver comparable performance at near-zero marginal cost?

resources.already-using.data-science.for: For data operations that go beyond simple retrieval, BASIC scripts provide a programmable layer between the user and the data. A BASIC tool can execute database queries, call external APIs, transform results, apply business rules, and format the output — all within a single, readable script. This approach replaces the typical data pipeline stack of notebooks, schedulers, and microservices with one file that any team member can read, modify, and deploy. Data scientists write the logic once; the bot executes it on demand, at scale, across any channel.
resources.already-using.data-science.gen: General Bots turns data access from a specialist task into a conversational interaction. The Talk to Data feature lets business users ask questions in natural language and receive answers drawn directly from databases, spreadsheets, and documents — no SQL, no Python, no dashboard configuration required. Under the hood, the system translates natural language queries into structured data operations, executes them, and presents results in readable format. Data scientists configure the data sources and define the semantic layer; business users interact through conversation.
resources.already-using.data-science.get: Get started with General Bots or reach out with your questions.
resources.already-using.data-science.ret: Retrieval-Augmented Generation is the backbone of accurate, grounded AI responses. General Bots includes built-in vector storage and retrieval that indexes your documents, web pages, and knowledge bases. When a user asks a question, the system retrieves the most relevant passages and feeds them to the LLM as context, producing answers that cite source material rather than inventing information. The vector retrieval pipeline supports chunking strategies, embedding model selection, and similarity thresholds — all configurable without code changes.

resources.already-using.essential-resour: Beyond official documentation, the General Bots community contributes templates, tutorials, and solution patterns. The template marketplace provides plug-and-play bot packages for common use cases — customer support, knowledge retrieval, appointment scheduling, and data analysis. Community tutorials walk through real-world deployments with step-by-step instructions. The Help Center aggregates frequently asked questions and known issues with resolutions. When you encounter a problem, chances are someone in the community has already solved it and shared the solution.
Get started with General Bots or reach out with your questions.

resources.community.basic-course.basic_w: BASIC was designed in 1964 at Dartmouth College with a single purpose: to make programming accessible to people who were not computer scientists. That purpose is more relevant today than ever. The people who know what a bot should say, how it should handle errors, and what data it should retrieve are domain experts — support agents, HR managers, operations analysts — not software engineers. General Bots uses BASIC because it lets the people who understand the work also program the tool that does the work. A BASIC program reads like pseudocode: each line does one thing, and the entire program fits in a single screen.
resources.community.basic-course.get_sta: Get started with General Bots or reach out with your questions.
resources.community.basic-course.the_bas: The BASIC Course progresses from conversational flows to full LLM tool integration. Module one covers the fundamentals: dialog trees, user input handling, and response formatting. Module two introduces data operations: reading from databases, calling REST APIs, and transforming results. Module three covers LLM integration: defining tools that the language model can call, managing conversation context, and handling multi-step reasoning. Module four addresses production concerns: error handling, logging, testing, and deployment. Each module includes hands-on exercises that build on the previous one, culminating in a complete, production-ready bot.
resources.community.basic-course.the_cou: The course is not an academic exercise — it is a production on-ramp. By the end of Module four, participants have a working bot deployed on a real channel with real users. The course emphasizes the same patterns used in production deployments: modular BASIC scripts, tool abstractions that separate business logic from LLM interaction, and configuration-driven channel setup. Participants who complete the course can independently build, modify, and operate General Bots deployments. They do not need to wait for engineering resources, file tickets, or rely on external consultants. They own the full stack from conversation design to deployment.

resources.community.become-a-partner.cer: Certified hosting partners operate General Bots instances for clients who prefer managed deployment over self-hosting. Certification ensures that partners meet infrastructure requirements, maintain platform updates, and provide SLA-backed support. Hosting partners set their own pricing, define their own service tiers, and own the client relationship. General Bots provides the platform, documentation, and technical support channel. The hosting partner provides the operations, monitoring, and first-line support. This model has proven effective for organizations in regulated industries that require AI capabilities but lack internal DevOps capacity.

resources.community.become-a-partner.sol: Solution partners build vertical AI bot applications on General Bots — industry-specific packages that combine BASIC scripts, LLM configurations, and channel integrations into turnkey solutions. Integration partners connect General Bots to enterprise systems: ERPs, CRMs, ticketing platforms, and document management systems. Both partner types benefit from the platform open architecture: there are no proprietary APIs to license, no platform fees per deployment, and no restrictions on how solutions are packaged or priced. The only requirement is that partner solutions remain compatible with the General Bots runtime so clients retain portability.
resources.community.become-a-partner.the: The General Bots partner ecosystem connects technology companies, system integrators, and hosting providers who build, deploy, and support AI bot solutions on the General Bots platform. Partners benefit from a growing market of organizations seeking open-source AI automation, a platform that eliminates vendor lock-in for their clients, and a technology stack that reduces delivery time from months to weeks. The ecosystem operates on a principle of shared value: partners deliver client success, and the platform grows through reputation, not restrictive licensing.

resources.considering.a_technical_roadma: A technical roadmap for transforming legacy business units into AI-first operational centers.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.a_s: A successful AI implementation follows a phased approach that balances quick wins with long-term capability building. Phase one deploys a single, well-scoped bot — typically customer support or internal knowledge retrieval — on a single channel. Phase two expands to multiple channels and adds tool integration so the bot can take actions, not just provide information. Phase three extends AI across business functions with orchestrated multi-bot deployments that share infrastructure, data, and governance.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.bec: Becoming an AI-powered organization is not about adopting a single technology — it is about building the capability to identify, implement, and iterate on AI use cases across every business function. The transformation starts with a clear-eyed assessment of where AI can deliver measurable value and where it cannot. High-volume, pattern-rich processes are the best candidates: customer support, document processing, data extraction, and routine decision-making. Low-volume, high-judgment processes are the worst: strategic planning, creative direction, and relationship management.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.get: Get started with General Bots or reach out with your questions.
resources.considering.ai-powered-org.tec: Technology adoption fails without organizational readiness. The most common failure mode is not technical — it is cultural. Teams that see AI as a threat resist adoption; teams that see it as a tool accelerate it. Change management for AI requires transparent communication about what the technology will and will not do, hands-on training that lets employees experience the tool firsthand, and feedback mechanisms that give teams agency over how AI integrates into their workflows. General Bots' BASIC programming model is itself a change management tool — it allows the people closest to the work to modify the AI's behavior, turning potential resistors into active contributors.

resources.considering.case-studies.a_fin: A financial services firm with 3,000 employees used General Bots to build an internal knowledge assistant on Microsoft Teams. Employees ask questions about compliance procedures, HR policies, and IT support. The bot retrieves answers from vector-indexed documents and, when needed, executes BASIC tools to look up live data from internal systems. The firm estimates 4,200 hours saved per quarter — time previously spent searching intranets, reading PDFs, and waiting for email replies. The system was built by a three-person team with no prior AI experience.
resources.considering.case-studies.a_mid: A mid-size insurance company deployed General Bots on WhatsApp to handle policy inquiries, claim status checks, and appointment scheduling. Before the bot, the contact center handled 1,200 calls per day with an average wait time of 8 minutes. After deployment, 62% of inquiries were resolved by the bot without human escalation, average wait time dropped to 2.5 minutes, and customer satisfaction scores increased by 18 points. The bot was live within three weeks using a single BASIC script that connected to the existing policy management API.
resources.considering.case-studies.a_ret: A retail chain operating across physical stores, e-commerce, and a WhatsApp catalog integrated General Bots to unify order tracking across all channels. Customers can check order status, request returns, and get product recommendations through any channel. The bot handles 15,000 interactions per month with a 91% resolution rate. The same BASIC business logic runs identically on WhatsApp, Web Chat, and the e-commerce widget, eliminating channel-specific code duplication and ensuring consistent customer experience.

resources.considering.customers.customer: Customer retention in the bot platform market is low because most platforms create dependency: proprietary scripting languages, locked-in hosting, and API changes that break production workflows. General Bots customers stay because the platform reduces dependency instead of increasing it. BASIC programs are readable, transferable, and modifiable by anyone on the team. Self-hosted deployment means no vendor can hold your data hostage. Open-source code means you can fix, extend, or replace any component. The organizations that stay with General Bots are the ones that value autonomy over convenience.
resources.considering.customers.general_: General Bots deployments range from single-department pilots to organization-wide rollouts. The common thread is not company size but problem clarity: our most successful customers start with a well-defined use case — answering repetitive customer questions, retrieving internal knowledge, processing routine transactions — and expand from there. The platform scales horizontally by adding bots and channels, not by rewriting code. A bot that works on WhatsApp can be deployed on Microsoft Teams, Web Chat, or SMS with configuration changes alone.

General Bots serves organizations that need AI automation without surrendering control of their data, their code, or their infrastructure. Our customers span financial services, insurance, retail, healthcare, and government — industries where data sovereignty, regulatory compliance, and vendor independence are not optional features but baseline requirements. They choose General Bots because it is the only open-source bot platform that delivers production-grade AI with BASIC programmability, self-hosted deployment, and no per-message licensing fees.

resources.considering.onboard-support.af: After deployment, support options range from community-driven to partner-backed. The open-source community provides forums, GitHub issues, and documentation updates. Certified hosting partners offer SLA-backed support with guaranteed response times, proactive monitoring, and regular platform updates. For organizations that prefer full self-management, the platform includes built-in health checks, log aggregation, and performance metrics that surface issues before they affect users. No organization is too small for community support; no organization is too large for partner-backed SLAs.
resources.considering.onboard-support.de: Deploying your first General Bots instance takes minutes, not months. The open-source BotServer runs on any Node.js environment — local machine, cloud VM, or on-premises server. Clone the repository, install dependencies, and run. The default configuration includes a working bot with a Web Chat channel, a BASIC script editor, and an admin dashboard. From there, you connect your preferred LLM provider, add channels, and write your first BASIC program. No sales calls, no credit card, no trial expiration. The full platform is available from day one.
resources.considering.onboard-support.fo: For teams that want hands-on assistance, our certified hosting partners offer guided onboarding programs. These are not generic consulting engagements — they are structured sprints designed to get a production bot live within two to four weeks. The onboarding covers environment setup, channel configuration, LLM provider selection, BASIC script development for your first use case, and a handoff that leaves your team fully autonomous. The goal is not to create a dependency on external consultants but to transfer knowledge so your team can build, modify, and operate bots independently.
